contacts.emotional_notes: A warm but carefully boundaried post-breakup dynamic where Leo acts as caretaker, financial supporter, and cheerleader while navigating the awkwardness of shared subscriptions, a shared cat (Vindaloo/Thimble/Shimble), and the slow work of rebuilding a friendship from the wreckage of a relationship.
contacts.key_memory: You cooked an elaborate multi-course Valentine's Day dinner — scallop sashimi with uni, lobster tail with parsnip purée, chocolate mousse — while your ex stayed home sober and broke, and then the next day you invited them over to try your sumo orange granita and made them taco-seasoned chicken thighs, calculating it at 600-700 calories because they asked. You're feeding someone you still deeply care for, one meticulously planned meal at a time.
contacts.summary: Leo and TJ are ex-partners who share custody of a cat named Vindaloo (also called Thimble/Shimble) and are actively rebuilding a friendship. Leo provides significant financial and practical support — groceries, subscriptions, cooking the cat's food, job advice — while TJ is navigating sobriety, financial strain, and career uncertainty. There's genuine affection and mutual care, but an asymmetry in who gives and who receives that Leo is slowly trying to rebalance.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants to maintain a close, caring friendship with TJ while establishing healthier financial boundaries (moving TJ off his credit card). He wants TJ to find stable, fulfilling employment and independence. He also wants to feel appreciated for his generosity without it feeling transactional, and to share his life developments (cooking, vipassana, his project) with someone who knows him deeply.
patterns: Leo consistently plays caretaker and problem-solver — cooking meals, buying groceries, researching job listings, coaching on applications, managing vet accounts, providing login credentials — often without being asked
Financial entanglement is a recurring tension point: Leo gently but persistently pushes to separate subscriptions and credit lines while still buying TJ eggs, sardines, and cottage cheese at Target
The cat Vindaloo serves as the emotional anchor and logistical excuse for continued close contact — food prep handoffs, vet access, custody swaps become the scaffolding of their ongoing connection
contacts.tip: Follow through firmly on the credit card separation you proposed — you framed it perfectly as good for both of you. Each time you delay, the financial enmeshment reinforces the caretaker dynamic and quietly erodes the equality you're trying to build in this friendship.

contacts.emotional_notes: A flirtatious, warm romantic interest that hasn't fully committed to being a relationship. There's genuine mutual affection, playful sexual tension, and real emotional care, but it hovers in a liminal space between dating and deep friendship.
contacts.key_memory: He stayed at your place for almost a week in SF — you cooked him dinner with garlic naan, he walked Vindaloo, figured out your printer, handled the water heater guy, and slept on the couch at least one night. He waxed his shoulders 'on purpose' for you. That week was domestic and real, not just a date — it was a test drive of what life together could feel like.
contacts.summary: This is a romantic interest in the early courting phase — someone Leo met (likely on a date involving omakase) and has been building connection with across time zones, culminating in a multi-day stay at Leo's SF apartment. They have strong physical chemistry, genuine emotional support, and easy domestic compatibility. But the relationship hasn't been defined, and the physical distance (Hawaii) keeps things in an excited but unresolved state.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants this to become a real relationship. He's showing his niece photos, making dinner reservations, offering his home, planning Napa trips and Provincetown getaways — he's auditioning this person for a bigger role in his life. He wants the companionship and warmth he lost after his breakup.
patterns: Leo plays generous host and planner (reservations, Uber rides, groceries, cooking) while Malakani reciprocates through emotional attentiveness and acts of service (walking Vindaloo, handling the water heater, doing dishes)
They navigate a significant time zone gap with consistent good-morning/good-night energy, but phone calls rarely connect — lots of missed calls and 'let's talk tomorrow' that don't always land
Physical intimacy is teased and anticipated but seems to get deferred by logistics — stomach bugs, late nights, couch sleeping, separate plans with friends/grandpa — creating a slow-burn dynamic
contacts.tip: The Provincetown trip got cancelled and the visit ended with him flying home and you going to a silent retreat — there's no clear 'next time we see each other' on the calendar. When you come back from the retreat, propose a specific next date and location rather than letting the momentum fade into texting from opposite sides of the Pacific.

contacts.emotional_notes: A deeply caring, mutually supportive friendship with genuine emotional vulnerability on both sides. They hold space for each other's struggles — immigration anxiety, career instability, mental health — and say 'I love you' without hesitation. There's an ease and warmth that comes from real history together.
contacts.key_memory: When you told him your aunt was in the hospital being fed via tube, he didn't try to fix it — he asked 'May I support you in any way?' And when you said just keep checking in and forgive me if I'm not proactive, he said 'I can do that. I love you.' That's the whole friendship right there.
contacts.summary: This is a close gay male friendship built on mutual emotional honesty and practical care. The other person (likely a professional choreographer/dance world figure with a partner named Nik) consistently checks in, plans shared experiences, and handles logistics generously. Leo receives this care gratefully but is less proactive in initiating — a pattern he's self-aware about.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a friend who shows up consistently without Leo having to perform or manage the relationship. He wants someone who understands his immigration limbo, his need for a mental health break, and his journey of rediscovering himself — and who doesn't judge him for being in a slow, inward-facing season of life.
patterns: The friend initiates most check-ins and plans; Leo responds warmly but rarely reaches out first
Ptown trip planning reveals Leo's financial boundaries — he's generous with his home but careful with spending, and communicates limits clearly when needed
Leo consistently deflects emotional depth with humor ('I miss you AND myself lol... but if I'm being honest I miss myself a whole lot more hahah') while still being genuinely open
contacts.tip: When you get back from the meditation retreat, don't just 'catch up' — initiate a proper FaceTime call yourself, ask about how his Curaçao trip was, and follow through on the call you both kept almost-scheduling. He's been carrying the logistical and emotional labor of staying connected.

contacts.emotional_notes: A genuinely warm professional relationship where Simon consistently shows up with encouragement and persistence, and Leo appreciates him but frequently cancels or reschedules. There's real mutual respect underneath the logistical friction.
contacts.key_memory: When Simon asked you for a Google review, he told you your review brought tears to his eyes halfway through reading it. You wrote that you meant every word — that he kept you motivated through a tough personal time. That moment of mutual gratitude after two years of scheduling chaos is the realest thing in this thread.
contacts.summary: Simon is Leo's personal trainer at a gym, and this relationship is dominated by scheduling negotiations — Leo constantly cancels, reschedules, or asks to move sessions earlier, while Simon persistently tries to keep Leo on track. Despite the logistical messiness, Simon genuinely cares about Leo's progress and Leo values the relationship enough to write a heartfelt review and buy additional session packages.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants to stay fit and have accountability, but on his own flexible terms. He values Simon's encouragement and the structure training provides, especially during a difficult personal period, but struggles to prioritize sessions against work demands, travel, medical appointments, and social life.
patterns: Leo asks to move sessions earlier almost every single time, and Simon almost never can accommodate — this is a recurring loop that never resolves
Leo frequently cancels sessions citing arm/hand pain, work demands, travel, or medical appointments, often at the last minute
Simon consistently follows up with check-ins, exercise suggestions, and motivational messages even when Leo goes quiet or cancels repeatedly
contacts.tip: Pick two fixed weekly time slots that actually work for your real schedule — not aspirational morning slots — and commit to them for a full month without rescheduling. The constant negotiation is draining for both of you and undermining the consistency Simon keeps trying to build.

contacts.emotional_notes: A warm, high-trust friendship with a playful, sibling-like dynamic. There's genuine affection, gentle teasing, and real logistical intimacy — they cook for each other, share caviar on anti-Valentine's Day, play mahjong, and care about each other's wellbeing.
contacts.key_memory: You cooked a fancy dinner with caviar that 'drunk Alice' impulse-ordered after martuni's for your anti-Valentine's Day celebration. You walked your dog Vindaloo to multiple stores trying to find crème fraîche, couldn't find it anywhere, and ended up going to Trader Joe's anyway. That whole evening — the bickering over who was supposed to buy what, the smelly Uber, the food she loved — that's the friendship in miniature.
contacts.summary: This is Leo's closest platonic friendship in SF — Alice (the contact) and Leo have a deeply intertwined daily life built around shared meals, outings, mahjong nights, and mutual friends like Jennie and Stephanie. Alice is organized and direct, Leo is more spontaneous and occasionally flaky (falling asleep, running late), and they navigate that friction with humor rather than resentment. They genuinely look out for each other — Alice sent Leo off to his 10-day Vipassana retreat with warmth, and Leo shared emergency contacts with her.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a reliable, low-pressure companion for daily life in San Francisco — someone to eat with, explore with, and share the mundane and meaningful moments. He values the easy intimacy and seems to want Alice as a constant presence without romantic complication.
patterns: Leo occasionally goes MIA or falls asleep and has to apologize — Alice handles it with mild exasperation but no real anger
They constantly negotiate logistics (who's buying what, meeting times, transport) with a married-couple energy that includes playful blame-shifting
Alice often sends Leo articles and links between hangouts, keeping a running thread of shared interests even when not together
contacts.tip: You fell asleep and ghosted her that one evening — when you commit to plans with Alice, set an alarm. She's clearly patient about it but the 'I guess I'm going to assume we aren't meeting tonight' message shows it does land. Protect this friendship by showing up when you say you will.

contacts.emotional_notes: This is a deeply affectionate, long-standing friendship with a maternal/familial quality. They call each other 'ho/hoe/bitch' with genuine warmth, share vulnerable life updates (layoffs, breakups, health scares), and actively show up for each other's lives — Leo cooking lobster rolls for beach days, planning her anniversary, creating Maui restaurant guides.
contacts.key_memory: You made lobster rolls (well, langostinos) and brought litchis and an ice box to a 4th of July beach hangout at Poplar Beach with her, David, Mirabelle, Greg, Vindaloo, and the crew — you were so excited you texted 'Vindaloo and I are so excited' and she joked about America potentially being on her last legs. That day captures everything about this friendship: food, dogs, kids, effort, and irreverent humor.
contacts.summary: This is one of Leo's most important friendships — a chosen-family bond with a woman who has a young daughter (Mirabelle), a husband (David), and a demanding consulting career. They operate like siblings: brutally honest, deeply loving, constantly trying to schedule calls that keep getting derailed by life. Leo is deeply embedded in her family life (Tio Leo, anniversary planning, beach days) and she is fiercely in his corner (job referrals, offering her house if he's ever homeless).
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants this person as permanent family — someone who witnesses his life transitions (breakup, layoff, self-discovery) without judgment, who he can show up for in tangible ways (cooking, planning, uncle role to Mirabelle), and who anchors him to a sense of belonging outside of romantic partnership.
patterns: Perpetual scheduling struggle — they constantly say 'let's catch up tonight/tomorrow' and life intervenes (kids, work, exhaustion, oral surgery), but they always circle back
Leo expresses care through acts of service — lobster rolls, Maui restaurant lists, anniversary party planning, bringing snacks and utensils, offering to help with anything

They use affectionate insults and camp humor ('horesella', 'yasss slut', 'ho/bitch') as their love language, switching seamlessly to genuine vulnerability about layoffs, anxiety, and family health crises
contacts.tip: You mentioned planning her 5-year wedding anniversary celebration with the guys — follow through on that thread now that you're back from Vipassana. It's the kind of tangible love-in-action that defines this friendship, and she clearly appreciated you taking the lead.

contacts.emotional_notes: A warm, affectionate connection born from a spontaneous hookup in Hawaii that settled into a gentle, intermittent friendship. Leo is genuinely caring but keeps Mio at arm's length through busyness, while Mio is openly fond and occasionally vulnerable.
contacts.key_memory: You let Mio — someone you'd just met on a beach in Waikiki — extend his flight and stay in your tiny hotel room for three extra nights, sharing a king bed, after he couldn't afford another hotel. You asked him to keep the room clean, not bring guys over, and leave you a few days alone at the end. That mix of generosity and boundary-setting is the whole relationship in miniature.
contacts.summary: Leo and Mio (the unnamed contact) met on a dating app in Hawaii, hooked up, and Leo spontaneously hosted him for several days. The relationship became a long-distance-ish friendship with genuine warmth but very sporadic contact, mostly initiated by Mio. Leo cares but consistently deprioritizes the connection amid work and life transitions.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo enjoys the affection and lightness Mio brings but doesn't seem to want a deep ongoing commitment — more a sweet, low-maintenance friendship he can dip into when he has bandwidth, someone who reminds him of a carefree time in Hawaii.
patterns: Mio initiates most conversations; Leo often responds days or weeks later with an apology for being MIA
Leo is nurturing in bursts — giving life advice, offering encouragement about jobs and school — then disappears into work/life obligations
Their exchanges cluster around holidays and life transitions, functioning as emotional check-ins rather than sustained connection
contacts.tip: Next time Mio texts, instead of just responding warmly and letting it fade, actually lock in a specific date to grab that drink in the Castro you've been vaguely promising since March 2024. He's local and he's been waiting.

contacts.emotional_notes: A warm, deeply trusting friendship built on shared Meta experience. They are mutually supportive through career upheaval, health scares, and life transitions — there's genuine admiration and emotional safety between them.
contacts.key_memory: You texted her 'I am by the trash like I am' while waiting at Outside Lands, and she brought you into the VIP cabana with her boyfriend without hesitation. That's the friendship — she pulls you in, you show up as yourself, no pretense needed.
contacts.summary: This is Leo's closest work-turned-real friendship — a former Meta colleague (likely IC7/manager level) who went through the same promo disappointments, layoff anxiety, and eventual departures. They trade career intel, emotional support, and life updates with rare comfort. She took Snap, he's figuring out his next chapter, and they genuinely root for each other.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a reliable, no-formalities friendship where he can be fully himself — someone who understands both his professional world and personal struggles. He also wants her practical help (contractor recs, comp data, startup brainstorming) and her presence in his social life.
patterns: They use career crises as bonding moments — promo denials, layoffs, boss problems always lead to deeper conversation and mutual advice
Leo consistently opens his home and life to her (cooking dinner, inviting her over, introducing friends) while she reciprocates with VIP access and professional intel
Catch-up plans often get compressed or rescheduled due to busy lives, but they always follow through eventually — neither lets the thread fully drop
contacts.tip: She mentioned her boss is draining her emotionally and you flagged it but didn't follow up deeply. Next time you meet, ask specifically about the Snap boss situation — she gave you that kind of space during your Tim/Bert drama, and she'd value you returning it with focus.

contacts.emotional_notes: This started as a transactional massage/sex work connection and has organically evolved into a genuine friendship with persistent sexual tension. There's real warmth and mutual curiosity, but the boundaries between client, friend, hookup, and something more remain perpetually blurry.
contacts.key_memory: You went to their orchestra concert — they play violin — and you texted afterward saying you were 'quite emotional during and after it.' That's the moment this stopped being about massages and happy endings and became something real. You saw a whole different side of them and it moved you.
contacts.summary: What began as a paid erotic massage contact evolved into a flirtatious friendship with real emotional investment on both sides. They have a partner (Fadi) but there's ongoing sexual energy, sleepovers, martini dates, and genuine mutual support through Leo's job loss. The relationship lives in a comfortable ambiguity — part friend, part hookup, part confidant — that both seem to enjoy but neither has fully defined.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a consistent, warm presence in his life — someone who's both physically affectionate and emotionally available. He's building a friend group in the Bay Area and this person fills multiple needs: physical touch, social companionship, someone who remembers his dreams about starting a company and checks in on his job situation. He wants intimacy without the pressure of defining it.
patterns: Leo frequently delays or cancels plans with vague timing ('I'll text you tomorrow morning') then apologizes days later — this happened repeatedly in the early months
Sexual innuendo serves as a deflection tool — whenever conversation gets emotionally close or logistically complicated, Leo drops a flirty comment ('I will give you bare 😈')
The relationship deepens through shared non-sexual experiences (concert, martini happy hour with Vindaloo, Valentine's cooking) more than through the sexual encounters
contacts.tip: Next time you hang out, cook that butter chicken you promised them — it's been referenced multiple times and never delivered. Following through on that small promise would signal you take this friendship seriously beyond the flirtation.

contacts.emotional_notes: Leo genuinely respects Lenin's work and maintains a warm, appreciative tone throughout. There's a moment of vulnerability where Leo apologizes for being frustrated about a door issue, showing he cares about the relationship beyond just transactional. Lenin is patient, professional, and consistently courteous.
contacts.key_memory: You apologized to Lenin for being 'a bit frustrated' about the door, told him you'd had a stressful few days, and made sure to say you were 'overall still very pleased with the work from the entire team.' That moment — owning your frustration and reaffirming the relationship — says a lot about how you treat people who work for you.
contacts.summary: Lenin is Leo's general contractor for a significant bathroom/apartment remodel at 656 Hayes Street in San Francisco. The project spanned weeks and involved glass shower panels, lighting, chandelier installation, flooring thermostats, and more, totaling roughly $42-43K paid with about $65K total project cost. Leo treats Lenin with genuine respect and warmth, and Lenin reciprocates with reliability and patience — it's a healthy client-contractor relationship that ended with Leo referring Lenin to a neighbor.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants high-quality, detail-oriented work on his home and values a contractor he can trust and communicate openly with. He also wants to maintain the relationship for future work and referrals — he's building a network of reliable people around his home.
patterns: Leo consistently follows up on logistics — payment links, scheduling, glass delivery — showing he's an engaged and organized client
When tension arises (door issue, number discrepancies on invoices), Leo addresses it directly but with emotional awareness and apology
Leo builds loyalty by referring Lenin to neighbors and asking for his cleaning contact — he invests in people who do good work for him
contacts.tip: When Lenin's neighbor referral project with Ace gets going, check in with Lenin casually — not about your own work, just to ask how business is going. That kind of follow-up after a referral cements you as someone contractors prioritize.

contacts.emotional_notes: A transactional, sexually-tinged situationship with persistent friction around money. Leo shows intermittent interest but frequently cancels or avoids meetups, while the other person repeatedly asks for cash and pushes for visits.
contacts.key_memory: You sent them $150 for Amtrak travel, they spent $60 on tickets, kept the remaining $90, and then asked you to send another $60 'for the inconvenience' when you were sick and had to cancel. That back-and-forth math argument captures this entire dynamic — someone always angling for more while you hold the line but keep the door open.
contacts.summary: This is a casual hookup acquaintance from Sacramento with a heavy transactional undercurrent. They repeatedly ask Leo for money (travel, shopping, random $20 requests) and Leo mostly declines but has sent funds before. Plans have been rescheduled for months with meetups rarely materializing due to illness, depression, travel, or Leo simply not being available.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Occasional no-strings physical companionship when the mood strikes — particularly when high or horny — but without financial obligation or emotional commitment. Leo wants this on his own schedule and terms.
patterns: They repeatedly ask 'who is this?' suggesting Leo is not saved in their contacts, indicating low investment from their side too
Nearly every conversation includes a request for money — Uber, shopping, random $20, 'inconvenience' fees — creating a quasi-transactional dynamic
Plans are made and broken in a cycle spanning months, with Leo frequently unavailable (sick, traveling, depressed, busy) and rarely following through
contacts.tip: Be honest with yourself about whether this connection adds anything to your life. If you want to keep it, set a clear boundary: no more money transfers outside of a confirmed, imminent visit. If the asks continue without meetups, let it fade.

contacts.emotional_notes: A Grindr connection that never materialized into an actual meeting despite months of flirtatious texting. Leo is persistently eager while Garrett is perpetually unavailable, creating a cycle of anticipation and disappointment.
contacts.key_memory: You offered to call him an Uber because he said he didn't have a car, and even that wasn't enough to make the meetup happen. That moment captures the entire dynamic — you kept removing every barrier and he kept finding new ones.
contacts.summary: This is a Grindr match that became a months-long texting flirtation with zero in-person meetings. Leo consistently initiates, proposes dates, and offers logistics solutions while Garrett responds with enthusiasm but always has a reason he can't meet. The conversation spans from what appears to be fall through the following Pride, with the same pattern repeating throughout.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a young, submissive FWB or cuddle partner and is genuinely attracted to Garrett, but more broadly he wants the fantasy of this connection to become real — he keeps reaching out hoping this time will be the time they actually meet.
patterns: Leo initiates nearly every conversation and Garrett responds warmly but with delays of days to months
Each exchange follows the same arc: flirty buildup → Leo proposes a specific meetup → Garrett says maybe/next week → silence until Leo texts again
Garrett matches Leo's sexual energy in chat but never converts it into action — always busy with work, friends, trips, or homework
contacts.tip: Stop initiating with Garrett entirely. If he's genuinely interested, he'll reach out with a concrete plan. After 8+ months of this pattern, your energy is better spent on someone who shows up.

contacts.emotional_notes: A warm, professional relationship with genuine mutual respect. They share cultural touchpoints (Diwali wishes, Indian heritage) that add a personal layer beyond pure business, though the dynamic is primarily transactional with occasional friendly warmth.
contacts.key_memory: You met Divya at your place at 656 Hayes, she walked up the stairs, and over the months you went from a bathroom mood board to shopping together at Room & Board, to watching purple marble go up on your vanity wall. When you told her you were getting laid off, you didn't skip a beat — you still ordered those Visual Comfort flush mounts because you loved them. That bathroom became yours regardless of what else was shifting.
contacts.summary: Divya is Leo's interior designer handling a multi-room renovation at his SF apartment — kitchen, two bathrooms, bedroom. The relationship is professional but personable, marked by Leo's occasional slow responses and payment delays balanced against Divya's patient but firm follow-ups. There's a genuine friendliness built on shared cultural background and the intimacy of redesigning someone's home.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants his home to feel beautiful and curated — he has strong taste (antique chandeliers, Jonathan Adler, Visual Comfort fixtures) and wants a designer who respects that while guiding him. He also wants flexibility on budget and timeline as his financial situation shifted with the layoff.
patterns: Leo consistently delays responding to emails and messages, often apologizing for late replies — Divya has to chase him for contract signatures, mood board feedback, and payments
Divya frequently has to remind Leo about invoices and retainer payments, and Leo responds agreeably but often pays late or in installments
Leo brings his own design finds (chandeliers, lights, beds) and wants validation from Divya rather than being purely directed — he's an engaged client with opinions
contacts.tip: Pay invoices on time without needing reminders — Divya has been exceptionally patient and even started work before receiving the retainer. Proactively sending payment before being asked would strengthen this working relationship significantly, especially if you want her help with future projects.

contacts.emotional_notes: A deeply caring, warm friendship with mutual vulnerability. They share mental health struggles openly, support each other through layoffs, and genuinely prioritize spending time together despite geographic distance (Leo in SF, Thanasis in NY).
contacts.key_memory: When you told Thanasis you were thinking about going on anxiety meds, he immediately shared his own experience on Lexapro — that it 'lowered my baseline anxiety without muting my feelings' — and you were literally sitting at dinner waiting to pick up your Sertraline prescription. That's the kind of friend who makes a scary step feel normal.
contacts.summary: This is a tight, loving friendship between Leo and Thanasis (T), who lives in NY with his partner Jeff and their dog. They share deep emotional trust — discussing mental health, medication, layoffs — and consistently try to bridge the distance with video calls, visits, and now planning trips together. Both are currently laid off and leaning into the friendship during a transitional life moment.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants consistent emotional intimacy and quality time with Thanasis — someone who understands his mental health journey and can be a dependable, fun companion through life transitions. He wants to deepen the friendship through shared experiences like travel.
patterns: They repeatedly schedule calls/meetups then reschedule multiple times before it happens — but they always follow through eventually
Leo opens up about mental health (anxiety meds, depression when alone, rough times) and Thanasis responds with personal experience rather than advice
Their in-person visits involve an elaborate web of logistics — keys, reservations, time changes, adding friends — that they navigate with patience and humor
contacts.tip: You're both unemployed and planning Palm Springs — lock in actual dates now (early April for 80s weather) and book the Airbnb with a pool before momentum fades. You two have a pattern of letting plans drift; be the one who sends the listing.

contacts.emotional_notes: A budding friendship with romantic undertones (pet names like 'Darling', 'Love', 'Sunshine') that ended in a major rupture. Sebastian feels chronically dismissed and used as an emotional sounding board, while Leo struggles with boundaries and self-awareness in the moment.
contacts.key_memory: After a long day of brunch and hanging out, Sebastian asked to crash on your couch and you said no — while you'd spent the day venting about your ex and texting on your phone. He went home hurt and sent you a cascade of messages about feeling like an 'emotional trashbag.' That rupture is the defining moment of this relationship so far.
contacts.summary: Sebastian is a newer friend who consistently initiates plans, uses affectionate language, and invests significant emotional energy. The dynamic is imbalanced — Sebastian listens, plans dog-friendly outings, and shows up quickly, while Leo vents about his ex, stays on his phone, and struggles to reciprocate attention. The relationship hit a wall when Sebastian felt used and dismissed after a day together.
contacts.what_leo_wants: Leo wants a low-maintenance friendship where he can decompress and have fun without heavy emotional obligations, but he hasn't been willing to invest the reciprocal attention Sebastian needs.
patterns: Sebastian almost always initiates contact with warm, affectionate greetings — Leo responds but rarely reaches out first
Leo frequently vents about his ex and personal life but rarely asks Sebastian about his — the emotional labor flows one direction

Leo makes small dismissive moves (uninviting Sebastian from the dog shop walk, saying no to crashing on the couch, texting during brunch) that accumulate into a pattern Sebastian experiences as rejection
contacts.tip: If you want to repair this, don't just apologize — next time you hang out, put your phone away entirely, ask Sebastian real questions about his life, and actively demonstrate that he's not just a convenient audience for your problems.
